input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Will update -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Android restarts the `Activity` on UI-mode changes.**
By default, Android destroys and recreates the Activity when the system theme changes. Prevent this by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges =
        ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode          // ← add this
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` declared, Android calls `OnConfigurationChanged` instead of restarting. You can optionally override it to detect the system toggle and swap your dictionary automatically.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two related .NET MAUI issues:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` for theme colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updated. They need `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the Android system dark mode changes, the Activity is recreated by default. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle the `ConfigChanges` flag to prevent this.

Let me give a concise, helpful answer.
